name: LOOMIER debug protocol — Stage 1 seed
description: Catalog of the deterministic Stage 1 diagnostics emitted by prototype/backend/src/debugProtocol/validator.js. Inspired by OpenGame's seed-protocol.json format (see third_party/opengame/NOTICE) but all entries are LOOMIER-native and operate on the declarative GameDefinition object, not on Phaser TypeScript source.

entries.title: Scene enables camera system but no entity has cameraTarget
entries.diagnosis: The camera system loops over entities that declare cameraTarget. Without one, the camera stays at its default and the player feels static.
entries.suggestedFix: Add cameraTarget: {} to the entity the camera should follow (typically the player). Zod defaults fill lerp/offset.

entries.title: Entity declares cameraTarget but its scene omits the camera system
entries.diagnosis: cameraTarget metadata is only consumed by the camera system. Without the system, the field is dead config.
entries.suggestedFix: Add "camera" to scenes[].systems, or remove cameraTarget from the entity.

entries.title: Entity transform.scale has a zero axis
entries.diagnosis: Three.js / Phaser render objects collapse to invisible when any axis of scale is 0. Schema only checks the value is finite.
entries.suggestedFix: Set every axis of transform.scale to a positive number, e.g. { x: 1, y: 1, z: 1 }.

entries.title: Entity model.scale has a zero axis
entries.diagnosis: Same as ZERO_TRANSFORM_SCALE but applied to model.scale specifically — the GLTF mesh will not render.
entries.suggestedFix: Set every axis of model.scale to a positive number.

entries.title: Mesh.size has a zero axis
entries.diagnosis: The primitive mesh will have zero volume on the collapsed axis and not render visibly.
entries.suggestedFix: Set every axis of mesh.size to a positive number.

entries.title: Two assets declare the same key
entries.diagnosis: AssetManager indexes by key; only the first asset with a given key loads. Subsequent declarations are silently shadowed.
entries.suggestedFix: Rename or remove the duplicate.

entries.title: Asset is declared but never referenced
entries.diagnosis: Unused assets bloat the bundle and confuse the AI on regeneration (it may re-reference a key it thinks is used).
entries.suggestedFix: Either reference the asset from an entity / audio rule, or remove it.

entries.title: Behavior action targets a missing entity
entries.diagnosis: setVelocity / setPosition / applyImpulse / destroyEntity / addTag / removeTag actions look up their target by entity.key. If the key does not exist, the action is a no-op.
entries.suggestedFix: Add an entity with the target key to a scene or prefab, or fix the typo in the action.

entries.title: Behavior action references an undeclared state key
entries.diagnosis: setState / incrementState / decrementState write to state.<key>. If the key was never declared at the top level, the runtime may either create-on-write or silently drop the update depending on the action handler.
entries.suggestedFix: Add the state key to the top-level state map with an initial value.

entries.title: Behavior action is not supported by the runtime
entries.diagnosis: Unsupported actions are dropped by normalization or ignored by the runtime, leaving the game without the intended rule.
entries.suggestedFix: Replace the action with a supported behavior action such as setVelocityX, applyImpulse, incrementState, destroyEntity, or switchScene.

entries.title: Initial scene is not directly playable
entries.diagnosis: The preview starts at initialScene. If that scene is a menu or lacks player, ground, or behavior rules, the generated game feels empty.
entries.suggestedFix: Point initialScene to the gameplay scene, or make the initial scene contain the playable player, ground collider, and behavior rules.

entries.title: Initial scene has no dynamic player
entries.diagnosis: A playable scene needs a player-like dynamic rigid body that input and camera systems can target.
entries.suggestedFix: Add an entity keyed or tagged player with a dynamic rigidBody, collider, visual component, and cameraTarget.

entries.title: Initial scene has no static ground collider
entries.diagnosis: Without a static ground/platform/world collider, a physics player falls forever or has nothing to stand on.
entries.suggestedFix: Add a visible ground, platform, floor, or world entity with rigidBody.type static and an explicit collider.

entries.title: Initial scene has no supported behavior rules
entries.diagnosis: The scene may render but does not have supported runtime actions wired to input, collision, timer, or state triggers.
entries.suggestedFix: Add scene.behaviors or top-level behaviors using supported actions. Do not place behaviors on entities.

entries.title: Tween references a missing entity
entries.diagnosis: TweenSystem looks up tween.target by entity.key. A missing target means the tween never runs and the visual stays static.
entries.suggestedFix: Add the entity with the target key, or fix the tween target.

entries.title: engine.enable3D is false but 3D content exists
entries.diagnosis: Three.js renderer is skipped when enable3D is false. Any mesh / model / light declared in the definition will be invisible. The schema normalizer auto-corrects this, but if this diagnostic still fires, normalization did not run.
entries.suggestedFix: Set engine.enable3D to true, or remove the 3D content.

entries.title: engine.enable2D is false but 2D content exists
entries.diagnosis: Phaser overlay is skipped when enable2D is false. Sprites and UI elements will not render.
entries.suggestedFix: Set engine.enable2D to true, or remove sprites and UI.

entries.title: engine.enablePhysics is false but entities have rigidBody
entries.diagnosis: Rapier world is not stepped when enablePhysics is false. Colliders are inert; nothing falls, nothing collides.
entries.suggestedFix: Set engine.enablePhysics to true, or remove rigidBody from entities.

entries.title: Entity has rigidBody but no mesh, model, or sprite
entries.diagnosis: An invisible collider can be intentional (invisible wall, trigger volume) but is often an oversight.
entries.suggestedFix: If intentional, ignore. Otherwise add a mesh or model.

entries.title: Collider was auto-defaulted because there was no sibling mesh
entries.diagnosis: The schema normalizer inserts a default cuboid collider when rigidBody.collider is missing. Without a sibling mesh to size it from, the default 0.5x0.5x0.5 is rarely the right size.
entries.suggestedFix: Declare an explicit collider.halfExtents matching the intended footprint, or add a sibling mesh to drive inference.

entries.title: Player entity exists but inputBindings is empty
entries.diagnosis: Without input bindings the player cannot move. Schema normalization usually injects defaults, but only if the player is detected via the key/tag heuristic.
entries.suggestedFix: Populate inputBindings with at least moveLeft / moveRight / jump.
